informed the audit team that adjustments pertaining to the stale checks were already
considered in the January 2023 bank reconciliation. Moving forward, she also

3.26 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require:
a. The Municipal Treasurer to immediately update the Real Property Tax
Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card based on the assessment roll and
furnish the Municipal Accountant of a duly certified list showing the name
of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as the basis

correct schedule of collections for RPT indicating the tax due year,
discount (advance/prompt) and penalty and the data therein should be
consistent in the summary of collection provided to the Municipal
Accountant.

book up the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year; (ii)
make use of the abstract of real property tax receipts in properly recording
the transactions pertaining to the collections of property taxes, and (iii)
prepare adjusting entries in order to rectify the noted variances of the
affected accounts.

3.27 During the exit conference, the representative from the MTO was amenable that
they have not considered the discount in the preparation of their report provided to

correct the errors.

3.28 Likewise, awaiting the list from the MTO, the Municipal Accountant will make use

3.29 Nonetheless, the representative from the MTO and the Municipal Accountant
agreed to conform to the audit recommendations and will draw necessary correcting

3.30 This is a reiteration of an audit observation in CY 2021-3 and remained
unimplemented to date.

a. Require the Municipal Accountant to:
i. submit detailed schedule of the debit adjustments made as the last
month of the year;
ii. prepare JEV to correct the misclassification of PPE to another
account;
iii. prepare the JEV to correct the erroneous recognition and non-
recognition of PPE account;
iv. ensure that depreciation is properly provided for each class of PPE as
discussed in PAG3 of the IPSAS 17 and provide for the detailed
schedule of depreciation; and
v. prepare and maintain property ledger cards and subsidiary ledgers of
each PPE account.
b. Require the General Service Officer to prepare and maintain property
cards for all PPEs of the Municipality and effect regular reconciliation

c. Require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical inventory in
three months or less, to prepare and submit the approved RPCPPE to the
COA Audit Team and ensure full implementation of “One-time cleansing
of PPE accounts” as provided for in COA Circular No. 2020-006.

started the inventory of various PPE of the Municipality and in the process of
recording the same in the property cards.

4.27 The audit team will closely monitor the compliance of the Municipality on this audit
recommendations as these are reiteration of the calendar years 2019-2 and 2018-2
audit recommendations which remained unimplemented to date.

properly classified; and

conduct of reconciliations between their respective records and every end
of the month thereafter.

projects during the year which includes the specific projects for lump

c. Municipal Engineer to observe compliance with the Revised IRR of RA
9184 on the issuance or grant of suspension and extension orders and
impose liquidated damages as a result of the invalid
suspension/extension orders; and
d. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and
submit accurate and correct report to oversight agencies for proper
monitoring.

7.27 All the concerned personnel have concurred on the audit observation and promised
to strictly comply with the audit recommendations. Also, a revised ordinance no.
2-2022 was submitted to justify the changes in the amount granted per utilization
of the cost sharing projects.

7.28 The LCE informed the audit team that they have requested from the DILG for the
retraining of their project monitoring team so that delays and low implementation
of projects could be monitored.

7.29 The audit team will closely monitor the compliance of the management because
said recommendations have remained unimplemented per reference to CY 2021-9,
2018-4, and 2017-5.

Receipt be issued for the receipt of In-Kind Donations and require the
following:
a. the General Services Officer- Designate, and the Municipal Health Officer to
coordinate in the preparation of the monthly reports on the receipt and
distribution/utilization/issuance of donated items and account for the
unutilized items at year end, if any and prepare and maintain a Registry
of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM.
b. the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary disclosure in the Notes to
FS as to the quantity of received, issued and balance of all donation-in-kind
received by the Municipality, with categorization as to food items,
medicines and medical supplies, hygiene kits, and others.

8.15 During the exit conference, the management accepted the audit observation and that
the proper logistics of the donated vaccines was not followed as it was directly
given to the end-user which is the RHU.

8.16 The audit team appreciated the timely reports of the MHO and the explanation on
some of the differences noted. Moreover, incident report was already made for the
remaining expired vaccines.

9.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require:
a. the Municipal Accountant to record the receipt of the LGSF- FA to LGU
in the trust fund as required by its guidelines;
b. the BAC and its Secretariat to be mindful of the procurement timeline and
submit written justification on the delay in the start of procurement of the
said project and the lacking documents as enumerated;
c. the Municipal Engineer to submit written justification on the delay in the
implementation of the project duly supported with documents.

9.7 This audit recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of the Notice
of Suspension if the personnel concerned failed to rectify the deficiencies noted.

9.8 The Municipal Engineer justified that they were late in the commencement of the
procurement process due to the failure of the Municipality’s financial managers to
inform the BAC about the Municipality being a recipient of said fund transfer and
actual availability of the fund. If only the Municipal Treasurer had informed the
BAC that the fund was available as early as the time the fund has reflected in the
bank statement, the implementing office could have acted with urgency, taking
into account that said fund has a prescriptive period of utilization. In addition, the
delay in the start of the construction was caused by the unfavorable weather
condition as expected during the last quarter of the year.

9.9 Nonetheless, Management promised to comply the submission of documents to
support their justifications on the delay in the implementation of the project.

9.10 The audit team reiterates the importance of regular monitoring of the funds and
close coordination with concerned personnel to ensure that planned PPAs are timely
implemented.

10.14 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. update files in the MTO pertaining to the real property taxes;
b. immediately send collection letters to the delinquent property owners;
c. prepare the complete Notice of Delinquency in the payment of the Real
Property Tax and post the same at the main entrance of the municipal hall
and in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay of the
local government unit concerned; and
d. provide explanation, duly supported with documents, on the basis of the
different assessed value provided in the real property tax receipts.

10.15 We likewise recommended and the LCE agreed to require the officials and
employees of the Municipality to pay their real property taxes promptly.

10.16 And finally, we recommended that the legislative officials, in consultation with
the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials to draw up plans to
strategize and strengthen the collection drive beyond the required posting and
sending of notices.

10.17 The concerned personnel promised the audit team that they will exert much effort
in order to provide full compliance on the audit recommendations. Also, the
representative from the MTO pointed out the lack of personnel in their department
that would focus on the preparation of the notice of delinquency including its list
and ask the LCE for additional personnel for their department. Also, she submitted
a written justification duly supported with CY 2006 assessment roll as this was their
basis for the computation of the tax due in the RPT receipts per instruction of the
Municipal Assessor since the provided assessed value in the CY 2019 assessment
roll for the noted properties in the observation were erroneous.

10.18 Full compliance will be properly monitored by the audit team.

Agreement (MOA) paid on a daily basis and period should not exceed 6
months; and
c. adopt a monitoring mechanism in order to avoid double payment of
services rendered and ensure that accomplishments be meticulously
evaluated and assessed prior to the renewal of JO contract.

11.17 The concerned personnel, during the exit conference, promised the audit team that
they will strictly abide with the audit recommendations

11.18 These are reiteration of the audit recommendations in calendar year 2018-6 and

submission of the report within the prescribed period; and
c. the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate registries of RAAO for
the QRF, MOOE, and Capital Outlay and closely monitor the charges
made to the special trust fund.

12.19 Commitment on the full compliance on the provided audit recommendations was
promised by the Management.

12.20 The audit team will closely monitor the compliance of the management since this
has been the observations for many years now.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the accountant to issue a written notice to concerned employees with unliquidated cash advances and demand settlement to employees pursuant to Sec. 89 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012, and strictly enforce the pertinent sanctions provided for in COA Circular No. 2012-004 including the suspension of salaries, if the demand remains unheeded.
observations.managementAction: Of the total amount of ₱3,710,000.00 the amount of confidential fund representing ₱3,710,000.00 already was liquidated. Also, reports were submitted to COA Central Office, and copy of the transmittal letter stamped received were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Of the total amount of ₱3,710,000.00, the amount of confidential fund already was liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly observe the provisions of Section 305 of RA 7160, the DSWD Department Order No. 22 s. 1995 and Section 4 of PD 1445 and ensure that supporting documents are present for grants of financial assistance.
observations.managementAction: The LGU of Daram has already submitted the Executive Order, and Approved SB Resolutions and supporting documents such as policies and guidelines for the grant of financial assistance to individuals.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management make the required disclosures in the Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements regarding the status of the LDRRM Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.

We recommend that management require the submission of the MOA, Program of Work, Project Expenditures, and other indispensable documents to support the release or eventual receipt of the funds by the beneficiary barangays pursuant to the requirements of Section 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommend that management obtain a Sangguniang Bayan ordinance authorizing the use of the funds for the implementation of specific projects chargeable against the lump-sum appropriation. We further require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that recording of fund transfers between GF and TF should be supported by complete documents authorizing the transactions.
observations.reason: No submission of required documents

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management clearly identify, prioritize and define the programs, projects and activities chargeable to the Special Education Fund and prepare the annual school board budget to guide utilization of SEF.
We recommend that all government properties should be provided with adequate government properties comprehensive insurance with GSIS.
observations.reason: No budget allocated for insurance of government properties due to lack of funds; management promised to provide a budget.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant be
required to submit the accounts and to strictly observe the timely submission of
the same in accordance with Section 347 of Republic Act 7160, Section 7.2.1(a) of
COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, and Section 39, PD 1445.

We recommend that management limit the use the 20% development funds
for purposes that are outlined in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2011-1 dated April 13, 2011. In no case shall, programs, projects and activities be
charged to the fund, unless they are included in the Annual Investment Plan.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer be required to maintain
a separate registries and subsidiary records for the LDRRMF and the Municipal
Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF for CY 2016 to a
Special Trust Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September
12,2012. Require also the Municipal Accountant to disclose information
pertaining to the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to
COA Circular No. 2014-002.

We recommend that the Social Welfare Officer formulate
policies/guidelines on the grant of financial assistance to individuals in crisis
situation and submit the same to the Sanggunian for approval. Likewise, see to it
that all disbursements are properly documented.

We recommend for the submission of the documents called for in the DBM-
DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-
01 dated January 8, 2016, to support the cash advance for Confidential Fund.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the timeframe for the submission of
disbursement vouchers for cash advance, with complete supporting documents as
provided for in the Joint Circular.

We recommend that the concerned officers and employees be required to
submit Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, Monthly Report of Official
Travels, and duly accomplished driver’s trip ticket pursuant to COA Circular
No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977.

We recommend that the management submit the list of all on-going
government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented
during the year pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor urgently require the Municipal Accountant to disclose complete information required under PPSAS in its Notes to the Consolidated Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Mayor Municipal Accountant
observations.reason: Submitted late, April 1, 2016

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to submit the reconciling official receipts and collections & deposit slips to support the recorded collections and deposits in the books. Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to draw a voucher to correct the understatement of the account in the amount of P39,531.13.
